recipe.title: Scottish shortbread
recipe.description: This Scottish shortbread recipe is brilliantly easy! Perfect with a cup of tea, or dip them in melted chocolate.

ingredients.name: lavender sugar
ingredients.note: use only 25g caster sugar in the main ingredients when making this version

ingredients.name: ½ a small bunch of fresh lemon thyme

steps.instructions: Preheat the oven to 170°C/325°F/gas 3. Mix the flour and sugar together in a mixing bowl. Rub in the butter with your thumb and forefinger, then add your chosen flavourings (if you’re using chocolate or seeds you might want to push these into the dough at the end, after you’ve rolled it out) and squash, pat and push it into a dough. Don’t knead it, you just want to pat it down flat.

on_enter.say: Preheat the oven to 170°C/325°F/gas 3. Mix the flour and sugar together in a mixing bowl. Rub in the butter with your thumb and forefinger, then add your chosen flavourings (if you’re using chocolate or seeds you might want to push these into the dough at the end, after you’ve rolled it out) and squash, pat and push it into a dough. Don’t knead it, you just want to pat it down flat.

steps.descr: Push or roll it out until it’s 1cm thick – do this...
steps.instructions: Push or roll it out until it’s 1cm thick – do this directly on to a baking sheet lined with greaseproof paper so you don’t have to move it. Once it’s in the shape you like – which could be square, round, or a few small finger-shapes – feel free to thumb or pinch the edges. If it splits or tears, just press it back together – but remember, the less you work the dough, the shorter and better these biscuits will be. If you want to score lines on the shortbread so that you can click the biscuits off into pieces later, you can.

on_enter.say: Push or roll it out until it’s 1cm thick – do this directly on to a baking sheet lined with greaseproof paper so you don’t have to move it. Once it’s in the shape you like – which could be square, round, or a few small finger-shapes – feel free to thumb or pinch the edges. If it splits or tears, just press it back together – but remember, the less you work the dough, the shorter and better these biscuits will be. If you want to score lines on the shortbread so that you can click the biscuits off into pieces later, you can.

steps.descr: Sprinkle over some caster sugar, then pop the baking...
steps.instructions: Sprinkle over some caster sugar, then pop the baking sheet into the oven and cook for 20 to 30 minutes. Keep an eye on it – you want a lovely light golden colour (unless you’re making the lavender & honey version, which will be darker).

on_enter.say: Sprinkle over some caster sugar, then pop the baking sheet into the oven and cook for 20 to 30 minutes. Keep an eye on it – you want a lovely light golden colour (unless you’re making the lavender & honey version, which will be darker).

steps.descr: Leave to cool, then put away in a tin or serve
steps.instructions: Leave to cool, then put away in a tin or serve. These will be delicious for two or three days and make a lovely present for someone special.

on_enter.say: Leave to cool, then put away in a tin or serve. These will be delicious for two or three days and make a lovely present for someone special.
